name: Remitly Global Remittance API
what_it_does: Remitly global remittance REST API for financial institutions, fintechs, and enterprises to integrate international money transfer capabilities, enabling sender-to-receiver fund delivery across 170+ countries with bank deposit, mobile wallet, cash pickup, and home delivery disbursement options. Enables AI agents to manage transfer initiation for cross-border payment automation, handle recipient profile management for international remittance workflow automation, access exchange rate retrieval for real-time FX rate display automation, retrieve transfer status tracking for remittance fulfillment automation, manage compliance screening for AML/KYC remittance automation, handle disbursement method selection for recipient preference automation, access fee calculation for transfer cost transparency automation, retrieve corridor availability for supported country-pair automation, manage transfer cancellation for remittance reversal automation, and integrate Remitly with financial institution, neobank, and enterprise platforms for end-to-end international money movement automation.
use_cases: Sending international remittances from cross-border payment agents
Checking FX rates and transfer fees from financial service agents
Tracking transfer delivery status from remittance fulfillment agents
Managing recipient disbursement preferences from international payment agents
not_for: B2B international wire transfers (use Wise Business or Airwallex for B2B FX)
High-volume treasury FX (use banking APIs for corporate treasury)
Domestic payments (Remitly is cross-border international focus)
best_when: A financial institution, neobank, or enterprise wanting AI agents to automate international money transfer for consumer remittance corridors through Remitly's global disbursement network across 170+ countries.
avoid_when: PARTNER API ACCESS IS NOT CONSUMER APP: Remitly partner API is separate from consumer app; automated B2C integration requires partnership agreement not consumer account; automated consumer-style API access without partnership creates unauthorized API access. KYC/AML COMPLIANCE IS MANDATORY: Remitly transfer initiation requires sender KYC verification; automated transfer for unverified sender creates compliance rejection; automated transfer pipeline must include KYC status check before initiation. EXCHANGE RATES ARE LOCKED AT TRANSFER INITIATION: Remitly FX rate is locked when transfer is created not when payment is made; automated rate display must reflect current creation-time rate; automated showing quote rate after order created creates rate mismatch. TRANSFER LIMITS VARY BY CORRIDOR: Remitly transfer limits (daily, monthly) vary by sender country and recipient country; automated transfer must check corridor-specific limits; automated universal limit assumption creates over-limit rejection in constrained corridors.

auth.notes: Remitly uses OAuth 2.0 for partner API authentication. REST API with JSON. Seattle, WA HQ. Founded 2011 by Matt Oppenheimer. NASDAQ: RELY (IPO 2021, $2B+ market cap). Products: Consumer remittance app, Partner API, Passbook banking. SDKs: None public. Licensed in 50 US states, FCA UK, EU AISP/PISP. AML/KYC compliant. 5M+ customers. Competes with Wise and WorldRemit for international remittance.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Partner program only; consumer app has promotional first-transfer offers
pricing.paid_tiers: Partner API pricing negotiated; consumer: transfer fee + FX spread (fee from $0-$3.99; FX spread 1-3%)
pricing.notes: Seattle WA. NASDAQ:RELY. Partner API pricing negotiated. Consumer pricing per transfer.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; transfer and rate quote APIs throttled; webhooks for transfer status events

known_agent_gotchas: PARTNER API REQUIRES FORMAL AGREEMENT: Remitly partner API access requires partnership agreement and onboarding; automated self-service integration without partnership creates no API access; automated standalone developer test access is limited to sandbox

KYC VERIFICATION GATE: Remitly requires sender KYC (name, DOB, address, ID) before transfer initiation; automated transfer pipeline must verify KYC status; automated transfer for unverified user creates KYC_REQUIRED error and transfer blocked

RATE LOCK ON TRANSFER CREATION: Remitly exchange rate locks at transfer creation time; automated re-quoting after creation will differ from locked rate; automated displaying post-creation rate as quote creates confusion when final amount differs

WEBHOOK FOR TRANSFER STATUS CHANGES: Remitly transfer status (CREATED, IN_PROGRESS, DELIVERED, FAILED) delivered via webhook; automated fulfillment must consume webhooks not poll status; automated polling-only implementation misses status change timing

CORRIDOR LIMITS ARE DYNAMIC: Remitly per-corridor daily/monthly limits change based on compliance rules; automated transfer must check corridor limits at runtime; automated hardcoded limit creates unexpected over-limit rejection
agent_readiness.security_notes: Global remittance. NASDAQ:RELY. OAuth2. US/EU. Financial transfer and identity data. AML/KYC regulated.

security_score_components.security_notes: Global remittance. AML/KYC. OAuth2. US/EU. Financial transfer and identity data.
